doctor.metaDesc: 22-year ICL lens implantation ophthalmologist, Director Lee Dong Hoon, international conference presentations, education and career
doctor.heroDesc: 22-year specialist in ICL Lens Implantation Surgery
doctor.heroQuote: We are committed to providing honest, high-quality care.
doctor.visionIntro: This is Dr. LEE DONG HOON from DR.ICL Eye Clinic."
doctor.visionBox1Title: I observed the clinical results of lens implantation performed for the first time in Korea.
doctor.visionBox1Desc: Over twenty years ago, when I first learned about ICL surgery, lens implantation was unfamiliar and new not only to patients but also to doctors. It was, to some extent, a nerve-wracking procedure for the surgeons as well. However, witnessing patients suffering from high myopia—a condition that isn't classified as a disease—who could not achieve proper vision correction with glasses or contact lenses, suddenly regain normal or even better than normal vision after just a single surgery lasting about 30 minutes, left me in awe. Even after 20 years, that memory remains vivid in my mind. Being a new and innovative procedure, there was not much information available in Korea at the time. This made it seem like a complex mathematical challenge to me as an inexperienced ophthalmologist, and coupled with the bright smiles of patients the day after surgery and the irreplaceable satisfaction I felt as their surgeon, it has fueled my continued research into lens implantation up to this day.
doctor.visionBox2Title: I published the first ICL paper by a Korean in a prestigious international journal.
doctor.visionBox2Desc: As a grateful reward for these efforts, my first-author paper on lens vaulting, which is a critical factor in preventing ICL complications, was published in Ophthalmology, a journal considered the top in the field of ophthalmic clinical research, making me the first Korean to achieve this. Considering that there were fewer than ten related papers on ICL in Ophthalmology at that time, I still feel it was an overwhelming honor, and I am always grateful to the patients whose support enabled me to achieve such good clinical results. However, it is disappointing that even after over a decade, with ICL surgeries now exceeding 20,000 annually, no follow-up papers highlighting Korea's exceptional ICL results have been published in Ophthalmology. As a researcher, I feel a sense of regret and recognize that I must continue to work harder.
doctor.visionBox3Title: I established DR.ICL Eye Clinic, the first ICL-specialized clinic in Korea.
doctor.visionBox3Desc: Vision correction procedures not only improve the quality of life but also have significant clinical importance by allowing individuals to live without glasses or contact lenses in emergency or challenging situations. As this understanding has become more widespread, ICL, which was once unfamiliar to many, has gained popularity, leading to concerns about safety and the prevention of complications. Personally, I have felt a vague frustration with the reality that lens implantation is often regarded as a secondary procedure under the laser-centric treatment systems like LASIK and LASEK. I have been contemplating the necessity of a specialized clinic equipped with optimized facilities and personnel for ICL, rather than relying solely on laser procedures. As a result of this contemplation, I decided to open DR.ICL Eye Clinic with the commitment to provide "the best lens implantation with the best facilities and the best service."
doctor.visionBox4Title: DR.ICL Eye Clinic is Korea's first specialized clinic for posterior chamber ICL with over 15,000 cases and optimized facilities for lens implantation.
doctor.visionBox4Desc: Through my 22 years of experience with various vision correction surgeries, including LASIK, LASEK, SMILE, and lens implantation, I can confidently recommend ICL as the best option. It is suitable not only for high myopia and astigmatism but also for patients suffering from severe dry eyes, corneal dystrophy, keratoconus, corneal shape irregularities, and those considering reoperation due to vision deterioration after laser surgery. ICL minimizes the shock to the eyes from surgery, making it the optimal treatment method. I have continued my research on ICL through lectures at domestic and international conferences and have experience with over 15,000 ICL surgeries. I have worked diligently to improve my surgical skills, reducing the average surgery time to approximately 10 minutes for both eyes, which is more than half the time compared to the past. This reduction in surgery time helps to alleviate the burden on the eyes and promotes early visual recovery while minimizing complications. DR.ICL Eye Clinic is equipped with advanced examination and surgical facilities optimized for ICL, allowing for same-day examination and surgery. Our experienced staff provides kind and calm one-on-one consultations to address any anxiety and concerns that patients may have before the surgery. Additionally, while the ICL and cataract surgery intraocular lenses are semi-permanent, we recommend annual post-operative check-ups to ensure long-term eye health. This applies not only to new patients but also to those who have previously undergone lens implantation, helping everyone maintain the health of their precious eyes over time.

equipment.metaDesc: Doctor ICL Eye Clinic Advanced Equipment - TOMEY CASIA2, ANTERION, i-Trace, IOL Master and more
equipment.sectionTitle: Advanced Equipment at Dr. ICL Eye Clinic
equipment.sectionDesc: Through state-of-the-art equipment and extensive surgical experience, we provide each patient with the safest and most accurate surgical methods and equipment, ensuring a secure and successful procedure.
equipment.cleaningTitle:
Dr. ICL Eye Clinic's
Six-Layer Sterile Surgical System
equipment.cleaningDesc: Our unique six-layer clean surgical system ensures 99.99% sterilization from the pre-filter stage with UV LED treatment, protecting against Staphylococcus aureus, E. coli, epidermidis, bacteriophages, and viruses.
equipment.equip0Medium: High-Resolution Anterior Segment CT Imaging Equipment
equipment.equip0Desc: This essential device is used for lens implantation, corneal surgery exams, cataract, and glaucoma surgeries, offering deep scanning depth (13mm) and faster scan speeds for clearer images. It enables ICL simulation, corneal topography + IOL selection and calculation, and lens shape analysis.
equipment.equip1Name: All-New CELLCHEK20 Corneal Endothelium Cell Analyzer
equipment.equip1Medium: Measuring the Function of the Corneal Endothelium, the Transparent Window of the Eye
equipment.equip1Desc: This equipment evaluates the function of the corneal endothelium, the transparent layer of the eye, which is crucial for maintaining corneal clarity. Decline in endothelium function may result from age, previous eye surgeries, or corneal disease. With a 40x magnification compared to slit-lamp biomicroscopy, it visualizes endothelium cells to measure cell size, density, and count, helping diagnose corneal disease and endothelial cell damage.
equipment.equip2Desc: Through wavefront analysis, this device conducts six types of exams in one go (aberration test, corneal topography, refraction, corneal curvature, pupil size, and tear film test), providing precise and objective pre-surgical assessments, including visual quality checks.
equipment.equip3Medium: High-Resolution Anterior Segment CT Imaging Equipment
equipment.equip3Desc: ANTERION utilizes high-resolution OCT imaging capabilities to provide anterior segment exams and measurements required for lens implantation, corneal surgeries, cataract, and glaucoma treatments.
equipment.equip4Medium: A Screening Device for Measuring Eye Refraction
equipment.equip4Desc: By simply focusing on the target inside the machine, it measures the overall refractive power of the eye and the corneal curvature of the front transparent part. This device helps identify refractive errors like myopia, hyperopia, and astigmatism, and is also useful in cataract and refractive correction surgeries
equipment.equip5Desc: This device allows for an enlarged view of the optic nerve and retina without uncomfortable pupil dilation, making it essential for diagnosing retinal diseases and glaucoma.
equipment.equip6Desc: Capable of tracking the moving retina and detecting subtle changes, this equipment enables vascular imaging without a contrast agent. It aids in the diagnosis and treatment of glaucoma, macular degeneration, diabetic retinopathy, and retinal vascular occlusions by assessing the condition of retinal and choroidal vessels.
equipment.equip7Name: Laser Eye Measurement Device - IOL MASTER
equipment.equip7Medium: The world's first and leading ultra-precise laser optometry device for cataract surgery, with laser technology offering 5 times more accuracy.
equipment.equip7Desc: This device measures the eye's refraction, length, and corneal diameter without contact. It enables accurate measurements for intraocular lens implantation, essential for lens implantation and cataract surgeries, resulting in outstanding surgical outcomes.
equipment.equip8Medium: Surgical Microscope with Over 170 Years of Heritage
equipment.equip8Desc: It is crucial to have a variety of equipment since different microscopes vary in magnification and depth of focus. Providing optimal surgical conditions maximizes the stability and precision of surgeries.
equipment.equip9Desc: The non-contact tonometer measures intraocular pressure by releasing a compressed air puff against the cornea and calculating the pressure based on the cornea's response. This device is fundamental in eye diagnostics for assessing elevated or reduced eye pressure.
equipment.equip10Medium: Advanced Device Ensuring Surgical Safety with Top-Rated Reliability
equipment.equip10Desc: As the first dual-pump system among domestic ultrasound devices, it minimizes endothelial cell damage and controls post-surgery astigmatism with low-energy ultrasound. Equipped with WhiteStar technology, it reduces incision site thermal damage, lowering the risk of post-surgical infection or complications.
equipment.equip11Medium: Fast, Accurate, and Effortless Visual Field Testing Equipment
equipment.equip11Desc: Equipped with GPA (Guided Progression Analysis), it detects early progression of visual field changes, crucial for glaucoma management. This gold-standard device ensures high precision in visual field abnormality diagnosis.
equipment.equip12Medium: A Surgical Microscope with Over 160 Years of Heritage
equipment.equip12Desc: This surgical microscope, crafted with Carl Zeiss's advanced technology, is designed for optimal viewing by the surgeon during procedures. It maximizes surgical stability and precision by providing the ideal surgical conditions.
equipment.equip13Medium: Precision Measurement for Lens Prescription and UV Protection
equipment.equip13Desc: This device measures the prescription strength and UV protection level of eyeglasses. Using Hartmann wavefront technology and a light loss-compensating algorithm, it minimizes measurement errors, delivering highly accurate results.
equipment.equip14Desc: This camera allows quick and efficient imaging of 200 degrees (approximately 80%) of the retina without the need for pupil dilation, enabling rapid assessment of both central and peripheral retinal areas. It facilitates early detection of peripheral retinal diseases (such as retinal tears and degeneration) that might otherwise go unnoticed. Without the dilation process, there is no waiting time, allowing patients to resume daily activities immediately after the test.
equipment.clean0Desc: Ensuring a sterile environment by filtering air with UV LED treatment from the pre-filter stage to eliminate 99.99% of Staphylococcus aureus, E. coli, epidermidis, bacteriophages, and viruses.
equipment.clean1Desc: Equipped with an H14-grade HEPA filter that captures over 99.95% of fine particles as small as 0.3 nanometers, this system ensures safe ventilation even on days with high pollution. Made with antibacterial materials, the interior of the device is rated at Antifungal Level 0 (indicating no mold growth), providing consistently clean air.
equipment.clean2Desc: Fresh air is continually introduced to maintain a comfortable and pleasant environment.
equipment.clean3Medium: Optimal Temperature and Humidity in the Operating Room
equipment.clean3Desc: The system keeps temperature and humidity consistent for the ideal surgical environment and limits bacterial growth during surgery.
equipment.clean4Desc: The air is purified throughout various hospital spaces, such as waiting areas and lobbies, to help prevent infections.
equipment.clean5Desc: This vacuum sterilizer uses high-pressure steam to complete sterilization within a cassette in just six minutes, ensuring thorough and fast sterilization.
